metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the actual comment text at _config_schema.py:262 and :278-281 is updated (not just the quoted paraphrase), and add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:81` ('default-OFF v1 rollout' docstring) to the edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan says to rewrite the adjacent comments in `_config_schema.py` ('no longer default OFF v1 / evidence gate stays inert until flipped manually'), but neither quoted phrase appears verbatim in the file. The actual comment at line 262 says 'Default OFF for v1 (expand-contract: ship off → validate on the dogfood corpus → flip in a follow-up)' and at line 278-281 says 'done MANUALLY by the operator only after 150b's discriminates_novelty eval has cleared its bar'. The plan's quoted targets don't match the actual text, which risks the implementer not locating or updating all relevant comment lines. Additionally, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:81` contains the inline docstring phrase 'default-OFF v1 rollout' which is outside `_config_schema.py` and is not listed for update.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192` to the explicit test-update list in the 'What' section, specifying whether the assertion should be updated to `is True` or restructured with an explicit `False` config (consistent with the 'keep off-paths via explicit False configs' guidance).
findings.finding: The plan's test-update guidance names only `tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166` (novelty_drop_active is False) as the stale default assertion to fix, but `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192` (`assert vc.remediation_mode is False`) is an equally stale assertion that will fail after the flip and is not mentioned.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, extend the test-update sentence: '…and tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192 asserts remediation_mode is False — update to True (mirroring the rising_floor fix) and keep the back-out test (test_config_coerces_keys) passing via explicit False.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope (or disclaim) whether flipping `novelty_drop_active` to True by default also intentionally activates the code-review region-gated floor (workflow_ops.py:608, gate_dispatch.py:720), or add an AC/NON-GOAL clarifying the intended behavior for code-review paths.
findings.finding: `novelty_drop_active` is a SHARED evidence gate consumed by BOTH plan-review (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:204`) AND the code-review region-gated floor (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608`, called from `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:720`). Flipping it to True by default will activate the code-review rising floor for all code reviews by default — not only plan-review remediation loops. The plan frames this as a plan-review-only change and does not mention, scope, or acknowledge this behavioral impact on code-review paths.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section or NON-GOALS: 'Because novelty_drop_active is the shared evidence gate, this flip ALSO activates the code-review region-gated floor (ADR 0037 / workflow_ops.apply_region_gated_floor) by default — this is intentional and was included in the operator authorization.' Or, if unintentional, note that a separate code-review-specific flag may be needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md`, `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`, and `docs/review-kernel.md` to the doc-update targets, updating their 'OFF by default'/'default off' language for `novelty_drop_active` to reflect the new default-ON state.
findings.finding: The plan enumerates only `docs/config.md` and `docs/plan-review-gate.md` for doc updates, but at least three other files contain 'OFF by default' or 'default off' claims specifically about `novelty_drop_active` and `remediation_mode` that will be stale after the flip: `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:55` ('default off'), `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md:92,167` ('OFF by default' x2), and `docs/review-kernel.md:124` ('OFF by default (gated on the shared verify.novelty_drop_active)').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` (the `_remediation_decision` docstring at ~line 81) to the comment-rewrite scope in the What section, updating 'default-OFF v1 rollout' to reflect default-ON.
findings.finding: The What section lists comment rewrites in _config_schema.py but omits the stale 'default-OFF v1 rollout' docstring comment in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:81`, which explicitly says `verify.remediation_mode` is 'off/absent (default-OFF v1 rollout)'. After the flip this comment becomes false and misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What bullet, append: 'and update the `_remediation_decision` docstring in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:81` ("default-OFF v1 rollout" → "default-ON; explicit false is the back-out").'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` to the doc-update scope in the 'What' section (and AC-3), updating its 'inert by default' / 'each is separately gated and inert by default' language to reflect the new default-ON state with explicit-false as the back-out.
findings.finding: docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md contains stale 'inert by default' / 'default-off' framing for both remediation_mode and novelty_drop_active (lines 88-89, 96) but is not listed in the plan's update scope — leaving a co-located protocol document inconsistent with the new defaults.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, append: 'Also update docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md (~lines 88-89, 96): replace "inert by default" and the default-off framing with default-ON language, noting explicit false as the back-out.' Mirror this in AC-3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC (and a corresponding test or proving command) that runs the rising-floor and/or region-gated-floor logic under the new default config (remediation_mode=True, novelty_drop_active=True) against a realistic fixture and asserts the floor actually fires as expected — not just that the default values changed.
findings.finding: The plan flips two flags to default True, activating the rising-floor and region-gated-floor machinery for all callers that use the default config. No acceptance criterion requires executing or asserting that the newly-defaulted code paths actually fire end-to-end under the new defaults — the only test update mentioned (test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166) changes a default-value assertion, and off-path tests are kept via explicit False configs. Green tests that were written against False defaults, plus updated default-value assertions, do not prove the activated machinery runs correctly under default-True conditions.
findings.location: AC1 / AC2; What section ('keep the off-path (triple-gate) tests via explicit False configs')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'At least one test in test_plan_review_rising_floor.py and/or test_code_review_region_floor.py exercises the floor logic with an unmodified default VerifyConfig() (i.e., no explicit True/False override) and asserts the floor output is non-trivially different from an equivalent run under VerifyConfig(remediation_mode=False, novelty_drop_active=False), confirming the newly-activated path fires.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the class name `VerifyConfig` (or whatever the owning class is) to the What section's description of the `_config_schema.py` change so the target entity is consistently named across both the work description and AC #1.
findings.finding: The What section names the file and field names (`remediation_mode`, `novelty_drop_active` in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`) but never names the class (`VerifyConfig`) that owns these fields; AC #1 then references `VerifyConfig.remediation_mode` and `VerifyConfig.novelty_drop_active` as if this is established terminology, creating a terminology gap between sections that an implementer must resolve by searching the file.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'flip `remediation_mode: bool = False`' to 'flip `VerifyConfig.remediation_mode: bool = False`' (and similarly for `novelty_drop_active`) so the class name is established in the work description before being referenced in AC #1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicit statement) confirming that existing behavioral tests exercise the True-path (remediation_mode=True, novelty_drop_active=True) floor machinery end-to-end, OR add a new test that does so, to ensure the newly-defaulted path is covered beyond a structural bool-value assertion.
findings.finding: The testing plan addresses updating False-default assertions and preserving off-path (explicit False) coverage, but never affirms that the newly-DEFAULTED True path (the convergence floor machinery actually running) is exercised by existing behavioral tests. The plan states the machinery was 'INERT behind default-False flags' — now it will run by default — yet no AC or test description confirms True-path behavioral coverage exists, leaving the defaulted path validated only by a structural 'bool is now True' unit check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / What section — test update description
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Existing on-path tests (run with explicit True configs or now exercised via the new defaults) cover the convergence floor and novelty-drop behavior end-to-end; confirm at least one behavioral test exercises each flag's active path as part of the default suite.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly acknowledge in the What/NON-GOALS section that flipping `novelty_drop_active` to True also activates the code-review region-gated novelty floor (workflow_ops.py:608 / gate_dispatch.py:710), and confirm this is intended or add a separate gating decision for the code-review path.
findings.finding: The plan scopes the novelty_drop_active flip to the plan-review rising floor, but `novelty_drop_active` is a SHARED flag that also gates the code-review region-gated novelty floor (`workflow_ops.py:608`, `gate_dispatch.py:710`). Flipping it to True by default will simultaneously activate the code-review floor across all repos — a blast-radius the plan's What section, NON-GOALS, and AC do not acknowledge.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
